candidates.stmt: Margot has lived in Alresford for 32 years and has been a local councillor for over 20. Margot cares deeply about Alresford and the Itchen Valley, working hard to help our communities thrive. She volunteers as an invertebrate sampler in our beautiful chalk streams and has been pressing Southern Water to protect our rivers.

Brian has lived in Winchester for most of his life, with family roots in the city stretching back generations. As a councillor for many years, Brian has focused his work on planning, environmental protection, and ensuring that development in the district is responsible, sustainable, and aligned with the needs of local communities.

Oliver’s Battery, Badger Farm and neighbouring villages are wonderful communities surrounded by our beautiful countryside. People here worry about the pollution of our rivers, littering, air pollution and noise.

I pledge to protect our nearby nature from ever increasing threats from unwanted developments like the one planned by the Church Commissioners at the former Bushfield Camp. This plan will harm the area that had been left alone for half a century. We will lose wonderful views and important habitats as well as space to walk and relax. It will create an extra 4000+ daily car journeys along Badger Farm Road towards Romsey Road and the M3. It is the wrong development for so many reasons.

Winchester needs to be much smarter when planning new developments!
I propose:
•	Only build for our community and not for the profits of developers
•	Develop only on real brownfield sites near the city centre
•	Build communities that include housing, public services, employment, retail and leisure facilities

I have been a campaigner to improve accessibility of Badger Farm Road junctions for many years. Plans have now been made. Now we need to get on to implement these.
I want to see more and better bus connections connecting Hursley, Compton, Olivers Battery and Shawford with Winchester reliably so that people who can’t drive their own car are not isolated.

I am excited to see how the new cycle buses to Olivers Battery school have grown over the last 2 years. Such a great way to start a school day! I’ll help to get more of these established to every school in our ward.

Remember:
1.	These are the last elections for WCC. 
2.	No need to vote tactically anymore. 
3.	Vote for the change you want to see.

I’d be very grateful for your vote!

Simone, a proud mum of two, is an experienced software developer and trained teacher living in Bishop’s Waltham. She cares deeply about the community and is passionate about improving health and social care for local families. She believes in practical solutions, strong local services, and working together to support everyone.

I have had the privilege of being one of your Winchester City Councillors for the past 11 years and with your support, hope to be re-elected again in May.

I am a retired Chartered Accountant who had offices in Bishop’s Waltham for 35 years.  I was a parish councillor in Bishop’s Waltham for over 10 years including 2 as Chairman.  I was involved in the Chamber of Trade and have been treasurer of Town Team since it started. 

With my 2 fellow Conservative City Councillors, David McLean and Michael Kurn and Rob Humby as your County Councillor, we have a strong team that works closely with Bishop’s Waltham and Durley Parish Councils and other organisations on many items. We look forward to continuing do so.

I have also helped many individuals with matters that have caused them concern and will continue to do this.  It is important that all residents feel they have easy access to a councillor when necessary. 

I am always interested to hear your thoughts and concerns, so please get in touch if I can help.

Best wishes,

Steve

I love living in Bishop’s Waltham, and want to work for our community, to improve our services, and protect our environment and access to nature.
As a Green I’m passionate about our public services and our access to nature. I want to make sure that our awesome community continues to be a good place to live where people can thrive.
I’m proud to be standing as a green candidate. The Green Councillors have brought positive change, and held the Lib-Dems accountable to the electorate. They have spearheaded greater rights for nature, supported and encouraged the greening of WCC, and increased council support for veterans, and I’m excited about the positive change I can help to implement if elected to join them.

Resident in Meon Valley for over 20 years, Malcolm is an engineer with many years’ experience leading large, multi-national industrial projects. Malcolm values the special qualities of the natural environment of the Meon Valley and will always seek to protect them. Addressing the climate emergency is both a global and a local responsibility that will require strengthened communities, bringing back services and local businesses. 

As a current Winchester City Councillor, he has worked hard to speak up for our area including successfully combatting over-development in our villages, protecting our local rivers and waterways and improved accessibility for our residents. He is looking forward to achieve even more for the residents on Hampshire County Council.

Hampshire County Council provides local services like schools, roads and social services. Our area (Meon Valley) has had a Conservative County Councillor for more than 40 years. Many people think it’s time for a change.

Green Cllr Malcolm Wallace came second to the Conservatives in the Hampshire County Election last time. Since then, the Green vote has grown massively across the Meon Valley, including winning two seats on Winchester Council with 63% last year! If this year’s County by-election was based on how residents voted in last year’s local elections, then the GREENS WOULD WIN.

For a strong, independent voice who will make a real difference at Hampshire County, vote for Green Cllr Malcolm Wallace!

Roger, a retired teacher, has lived in the Meon Valley for most of his working life and in Swanmore for the past twenty years. He has represented Shirrell Heath, Shedfield and Curdridge on Winchester District Council for six years, and gained a reputation for being hard-working, and fighting to protect our rural communities. He is a regular participant in Speedwatch initiatives and was one of the organisers of the Stop the Quarry Campaign in Shedfield.

Martyn has worked in education in various roles, including college principal and now as an education consultant. He joined the Labour Party because he believes in equality, justice and fairness. Martyn has been Labour Party Secretary and Chair and was a councillor for Bridgemary North. He wants to represent all residents of Bridgemary and fight for the distinctive nature of the community. He believes that Gosport has been wrecked by the Conservatives: local democracy has been damaged by weak leadership and a lack of vision. Only Labour has the vision and the ability to turn things around.

Sophia has lived in the area for 20 years. She graduated from Durham University and upon her return is passionate to work hard and represent the people of Twyford and Colden Common to make the area the best it can be. She is determined to protect local buses and joining Danny Chambers MP in a fight to stop Conservative bus cuts.

She is passionate about the environment and has joined the fight for a greener future, where sewage is removed from rivers and one that strongly encourages solar panels. She is proud of the work Lib Dem Winchester City Council has done to increase recycling rates, including the rollout of food collection bins.

She deeply understands the issues facing young people and hopes to advocate for new jobs in the local area.

Martin is a lifelong Denmead resident and Parish Councillor, dedicated to serving his community. He works in adult health services, supporting people across the area, and brings a practical, caring approach to local issues. Martin is campaigning to protect services, support residents and give Denmead a strong voice on the council.

I am delighted to have been re-selected to stand for the Denmead ward of Winchester City Council in the May 2022 local elections. 

In the six years since I was first elected, I have learned more than I ever imagined and have participated in a truly diverse range of projects, including attending an archaeological dig and chairing the Newlands Arts Panel from which the park at Newlands evolved.

In April 2020 I began working for the NHS as a Social Prescriber, supporting the social and emotional needs of patients and building strong links with local organisations and charities.  I have been helping in the vaccination centre at Waterlooville Health Centre and ensuring our housebound patients were also vaccinated.  I have used my knowledge of both roles together to offer more holistic support to residents and patients.

My direct work for you, the residents of the Denmead Ward, has been equally wide ranging and worthwhile.

I ask for your continuing support on May 5th. 

Best wishes,

Caroline

Neil spent his working career as a tenant farmer on the Southwick estate where he was brought up. He played a major role as a local and regional representative in the National Farmers Union.  He has been a Winchester District Councillor representing Southwick and Wickham for over 10 years. He is currently Deputy Leader of the Council having played a key role in sustaining the financial viability of the Council in recent years.

I’ve lived just outside Wickham for 21 years, moving here from Reading when my wife and I had our first child. This is our home.

I joined Reform in 2025 and I’m running for election for the first time. Why you ask? To put it bluntly I’ve had enough of voting for parties who clearly don’t listen, so I’ve decided to be part of the change in direction our country needs.

I work in IT and use Fareham station to attend meetings across the country. I used to drive more regularly but travelling on the roads has become impossible due to the terrible congestion. I worry about the future of our community where just doing the basics of getting to work, shopping locally, running a business and raising our children is getting harder and harder.

Local council decisions don’t appear to be helping the situation and in some cases are making it worse! Plans lacking common sense driven by ideas which are not fully thought out with no clear direction. To top it off, all this change costs money, our money.

I have lived in the area for over 40yrs. Working for Barclays Bank in Wickham Square until starting a family. I have been active in many organisations  such as, School Governor, Playgroup Leader, Akers and Parish Councillor. I have helped to set up a Repair Cafe and a Greening Campaign Group. 

In recent years, I do not feel our rural way of life has been supported by the Lib Dem city centric council. I’d like to see more local opinions, experience and local knowledge being considered before planning, housing it highest decisions are made. 

I believe it’s time local people have a part in local decisions/policy, they are the ones who know the area best. We deserve better than we’ve been getting, so let me out your views forward to those that will listen. Southern Parishes have a Lib Dem controlled City Council who overlook our rural needs. 

A vote for me will be a step closer to your voice being heard. I will endeavour to assist with all types of village issues and to help you get your problems resolved , not ignored. 

Contact Denise Searle  07731867276 denisesearle-wickhamconservatives@outlook.com

Ben is standing for election in May because he believes that more Green voices are needed to champion the needs of _all_ residents, and campaign for stronger environmental action.

Ben knows that Green priorities – on addressing inequalities and the cost of living, and the climate and environmental crises – reflect the priorities of most people in Southwick & Wickham, and Southern Parishes, offering hope instead of division or managed decline.

Ben has extensive experience researching social and employment issues, developing policy that positively impacts people’s lives. He will bring these skills to his work as a district and county councillor, focusing on developing a dialogue with residents to understand their needs and to campaign for positive change. He will bring residents into the heart of the conversation and help them take action alongside him, coming together to get more involved in the politics that really mean something, not just social media outrage or pointing at potholes.

James Batho has been a hardworking City Councillor for many years and promises to continue his commitment to the local community on the County Council. A former teacher and youth group leader, he has lived with his family in Winchester for over 25 years.

Jessica has lived in Weeke for twelve years now and in Winchester for over twenty. Over those twenty years she has worked in local government, healthcare, and now in Higher Education in Winchester, where she is a lecturer on health and social care professional programmes.

She is passionate about supporting health and social care access for all, recognising the impact that local and national policy has had on the wellbeing of the community and workforce. Working in local GP practices she saw first hand how healthcare is more than simply the NHS, it is about housing, jobs, access to food, green spaces, and community. To this end she works to put the community and residents of Winchester at the centre of policy and decision making.

I am a full time mum and volunteer and I currently live in Winchester. I moved to Hampshire 10 years ago, after studying Sociology and Education and completing a PhD at Cardiff University. I have previously worked as a research assistant on various projects for universities and third sector organisations.

During the pandemic, when I stepped back from work to help my children learn at home, I got involved in fundraising for my children's school and became a parent governor. I also help supervise children on school trips.

When I talk to other school governors from across Hampshire at training events, I hear a lot of the same desperate stories. Parents are waiting years for suitable school places, children and young people cannot access mental health support, budgets are squeezed and headteachers are struggling to recruit and retain the quality staff they need to tackle educational disadvantage across the county. Schools are literally falling apart and it is no wonder when we have had six Tory Education Secretaries in the last five years.

We need a Labour government to put an end to this chaos and remove barriers to opportunity. The last Labour government played a pivotal role in raising aspirations for people from working class families like myself. I am therefore absolutely honoured to be chosen as the Labour candidate for East Hampshire.

Kathleen is your hardworking local councillor who gets things done, campaigning for safer, more family friendly streets. She has lived in Winchester for many years and has served as chair of Town Forum, helping to deliver many improvements to community facilities across the area.

Lorraine lives in the St Bartholomew area, giving her a first-hand understanding of the challenges and opportunities facing Winchester residents every day.

She is standing for election to represent her community and to provide a strong Green voice on the issues that matter most locally. Her priorities include improving access to safe and reliable transport, protecting the environment, and safeguarding Winchester’s fragile rivers.

In St Bartholomew, where she lives, the future of the former River Park Leisure Centre site is a key concern. Lorraine is passionate about seeing this space redeveloped in a way that supports the physical and mental wellbeing of residents.

Lorraine is deeply involved in city life. She volunteers with a local charity and serves as a tour guide at Winchester Cathedral. She also regularly leads historical walks around the city and surrounding areas, sharing her enthusiasm for Winchester’s rich heritage.

Lorraine says:
"I hear growing concerns about our throwaway culture. People tell me they want to see practical, local initiatives to reduce waste and encourage reuse. They’re also calling for stronger action to support walking, cycling, and reliable public transport, along with more accessible and affordable renewable energy."
"By truly listening to our communities and working through the political system, the Green Party can help drive meaningful change on these urgent issues."
Local campaigner and former City Councillor for St Michael Ward, Winchester City Council

Mark is your hard-working councillor in St Michael. He has lived in the area for 15 years, and currently lives in Bar End with his partner. He’s passionate about tackling the climate crisis and improving public transport, walking and cycling options for local residents in central Winchester.

As a lifelong resident of Winchester, I care deeply about the future of our city and the wellbeing of everyone who calls it home.

My family has lived here since the mid-1800s, and I’ve spent my life working, learning, and giving back to the community that raised me.

Many of you will know me from my 25 years working at NatWest on the High Street – a role that gave me real insight into the day-to-day concerns of local people and businesses.

Today, I run a small consultancy and spend much of my time helping residents navigate housing, benefits, and licensing issues. I'm also proud to support our local schools as a long-standing governor, currently at All Saints in Highcliffe.

For over 40 years, I’ve been involved with the Royal British Legion and served with the Wessex Yeomanry. My work with the Winchester Housing Trust, and formerly as a trustee of St John’s and St Cross, reflects my strong belief in supporting our city’s most vulnerable.

I'm especially passionate about ensuring affordable housing is available for those who need it – an issue I champion as treasurer of the Winchester Tenants’ and Residents’ Association.

Having served as a local councillor, I know how local government works – and more importantly, how to make it work for you. I’m here to listen, to act, and to stand up for what matters most to our community.

Some say I’m a bit of a maverick – and that may be true – but I’ve always stood by my principles as a proud Conservative who puts people first.

I look forward to continuing the conversation with you.

We are in a climate and biodiversity emergency. Local services have been cut and we have lost so much. I worry about what the future will be like for my grandchildren. I would like them to be able to paddle in the sea and local streams without fear of becoming sick. I’d like them to breathe cleaner air and be surrounded by more green spaces and nature.

To be able to cycle more safely and to be able to afford to live here when they are older. We need to vote more greens into positions of power and influence in order to take action now for a greener, fairer world and a future we can all look forward to.

Lucille is a hard working local councillor for St Paul Ward who has been fighting for safer crossings and speed reduction across the area. She has lived in Winchester with her family for 20 years and is passionate about keeping St Paul's and Winchester a special place to live.

Jackie has stood up for residents on local councils for over 20 years. She has helped hundreds of people and works tirelessly to help our communities in The Worthys, Worthy Down, and Kings Barton to thrive. Jackie is Chairman of the Tubbs Hall, a Governor at Kings Worthy School, and a trustee of The Watercress Way.

Born in Winchester and living in the Worthys and Itchen Valley since 2018 I combine over 35 years of business experience with a strong record of local service. I am a parent of two, a charity trustee, parish councillor and former school governor. I bring real life experience, practical judgement and a deep commitment to supporting residents.

Luke has been an active Green Party member for three years and is passionate about ensuring everyone in our community has a voice.  He has lived in Winchester all his life, moving from the centre of the city to Kings Worthy six years ago. 
Luke plays an active role in the local community and was co-opted as a Councillor onto the Kings Worthy Parish Council in November 2025 where he is a member of the Recreation & Amenities and Planning & Highways Committees.  He regularly volunteers, for example helping at a local fundraising event for the Winchester Hospice and planting a new hedgerow for Eversley Park, and is keen to get involved in a lot more in the future.
“I’m concerned that public trust is at all time low, and many people feel that they are not being heard. I always try to listen to everyone’s concerns and find a solution.”

David has lived in Meonstoke with his family for 19 years. He is passionate about developing local communities and making a real difference to the area. He is a member of the Parish Council and runs several volunteer groups. He knows how to get things done. When he worked in the telecoms industry, he successfully helped bring high-speed broadband to the area.

Dear Fellow Resident,
It has been my privilege to serve as your Councillor for the Upper Meon Valley ward over the past four years.  Many of you know me well by now and know I have put my heart and soul into representing your interests and helping find solutions to local problems.  I have greatly enjoyed working across the ward and would very much like to continue, seeking re-election, as well as stand for Hampshire County Council, in the Bishop’s Waltham Division.
For those residents less familiar with me, I am a science graduate with a 30 year business career, living locally in Bramdean with my wife and family.  I am committed to using that experience to deliver practical solutions to the issues we all face - potholes, flooding, sewerage, speeding and noise, protecting our countryside and supporting our rural businesses while spending your council tax wisely.
I believe in practical action and when Bishop’s Waltham tip faced possible closure, I met with Cllr Nick Adams-King, leader of Hampshire County Council, to stress its importance, helping secure its future.
There are many challenges ahead, but I care deeply about our community and will continue working hard to make our area an even better place to live and work.  I hope you will support me on 7th May.
If you would like to raise any issue, then please do get in touch.
With very best wishes
Neil

Anne has represented Whiteley and Shedfield on Winchester City Council for the past four years. Anne brings her commercial background to her work with a determination to see local services improved. Her priorities have been to protect village communities while working to ensure that facilities are provided for the expanding Whiteley community to avoid pressure on nearby areas.

I'm excited to be your Green Party candidate, and as a councillor would:

- Ensure the council works in the interest of people and planet 
- Involve residents & small businesses in decision-making and planning 
- Begin returning assets back into the hands of the local community

It's important to me that our councillors don't just work when they're up for election, and that they're involved and integrated with the community in which they live.

More Green voices on our councils is the best chance we have for a hopeful future!

Andy has lived in Micheldever for 18 years and works as a railway operations manager. Having served as a Parish Councillor since 2014 and as Planning Chair since 2022, he has experience of getting things done and delivering under challenging circumstances. On Winchester City Council, Andy will fight for quality services for all residents.
